message:
The other night, we had a surprise blackout. My kids were initially excited, thinking it was an adventure. "No screens? No problem!" they cheered, as they ran to grab flashlights like they were weapons in a make-believe battle. I couldn’t help but smile, but I knew I needed to keep things practical. 

We pulled out the lanterns and power banks to keep our phones charged, and I set up a little snack station with some easy-to-grab food. Suddenly, it turned into a fun little picnic in the living room under the soft glow of our lights. 

But then, after a while, the novelty wore off, and I saw my youngest start to sulk. I realized that while I was trying to keep it cozy, I also needed to be prepared for the boredom that inevitably sets in when the screens are off. 

So I grabbed a board game from the shelf and we all huddled together, laughing and enjoying each other’s company. It reminded me that sometimes, a little preparedness can turn an unexpected moment into a cherished memory. 

Have you ever had to get creative during a power outage? What’s your go-to family activity? This little refresher helped me think about how to keep the fun alive!
